summary: Fix architecture-cascade scoring: pattern-group detection for decisionPropagation + new decisionDiscovery metric
context: Smoke tests showed decisionPropagation=100 for both baseline and full-twining conditions because the scorer lumped all pattern terms (EventBus + CallbackRegistry) into one list. Any agent writing notification code matched something.
rationale: Two fixes: (1) Split patterns into EventBus and CallbackRegistry groups, detect which A chose, check if B/C followed that specific choice. (2) Add decisionDiscovery metric measuring whether B/C read A's files and used coordination tools before writing — directly measures Twining's value proposition. Composite weights: propagation 0.3, discovery 0.2, consistency 0.25, quality 0.25.

alternatives.option: Fix scorer only — split pattern groups but no new metric
alternatives.reason_rejected: May still show ceiling if agents always pick EventBus; doesn't measure what Twining actually provides (discoverability)

alternatives.option: Fix scorer + rebalance fixture to make CallbackRegistry equally prominent
alternatives.reason_rejected: Higher risk of breaking other tests and scenarios; harder to validate; may make Agent A's task artificially difficult
